# CoVE-vs-SelfCheckGPT-analysis-on-LLM-hallucinations
A research project to analyze whether SelfCheckGPT and CoVe complement each other in hallucination detection in LLMs when it comes to coding and Computer Science.


**Independent undergrad research project** exploring whether SelfCheckGPT (detection) and CoVe (mitigation) complement each other on coding answers generated by the LLM.

##The Research papers used and their summaries:
- The Research papers analyzed and implemented in this project are :
    - [SelfCheckGPT: Zero-Resource Black-Box Hallucination Detection for Generative Large Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.08896)
    - [Chain-of-Verification Reduces Hallucination in Large Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.11495)
- My summaries of each of the papers are available in the /summaries folder

## Overview
- Generated 30 coding questions using Groq (Llama-3.3-70B)
- Produced baseline code answers
- Ran **SelfCheckGPT-BERTScore** = flagged 27/30 (90%) as inconsistent
- Applied **CoVe** on flagged answers = 27/27 successful revisions

## Key Findings:
- SelfCheckGPT flagged most answers as suspicious because correct code can be written in many different styles = **high false positives**.
- CoVe improved the flagged answers by adding safety checks (type validation, error handling)
- **They complement each other**: Detection finds potential problems cheaply ; Mitigation fixes them selectively.

## Files & Notebooks
- `revised_original_dataset.csv` → 30 questions + baselines + manual answers
- `result_detection.csv` → SelfCheckGPT scores & flags
- `results_mitigation.csv` → CoVe revised answers
- `figures/` → graphs
- `notebooks/` → all code

## How to reproduce
1. Open notebooks in Colab
2. Install `groq`, `bert-score`, `spacy`
3. Add your Groq API key in Secrets
4. Run in order: 01 → 02 → 03 → 04

## Limitations
- Small dataset (30 questions)
- SelfCheckGPT over-flags correct code
- API rate limits interrupted some runs
